activities_projects.title: Local Market Visit
activities_projects.objective: Observe the functioning of a nearby market
steps: Visit a nearby market or shop
Observe different types of goods sold and the arrangement of shops
Note the interactions between buyers and sellers

activities_projects.title: Price Comparison Chart
activities_projects.objective: Learn about price variations in different markets
steps: Select 5 common items (e.g., vegetables, fruits, grains)
Record their prices in at least two different markets
Draw a chart comparing the prices
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand price differences across markets

activities_projects.title: Goods and Services Sorting Activity
steps: List items observed in markets
Categorize them into goods and services
Add small illustrations for each category

activities_projects.title: Role Play: Buyer and Seller
steps: Divide students into buyers and sellers
Simulate market transactions using play money
Observe how bargaining happens and record experiences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn negotiation and market behavior
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and realism in role play (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Market Vocabulary Crossword
activities_projects.objective: Reinforce key terms related to markets
steps: List 10 key terms such as price, goods, services, barter, customer
Create a crossword or word search puzzle
Exchange puzzles with classmates to solve
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students strengthen vocabulary related to the chapter

activities_projects.title: Market Poster
steps: Draw a poster showing a market scene
Include shops, buyers, sellers, and goods
Write short captions for different elements
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to visually present market elements

activities_projects.title: Barter System vs Money Transactions
activities_projects.objective: Compare traditional and modern trade systems
steps: Write 3 points about barter trade
Write 3 points about money-based trade
Highlight differences and advantages of each system
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand evolution of trade and markets

activities_projects.title: Story Writing: A Day at the Market
steps: Imagine spending a day at a local market
Write a story describing buying, selling, and interactions
Include observations about prices, goods, and services
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop understanding of markets through imagination
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relevance to chapter concepts (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Market Observation Table
activities_projects.objective: Record key features of different markets
steps: Visit two or more markets (local, weekly, or weekly haat)
Make a table comparing number of shops, types of goods, and crowd
Analyze which market is busier and why
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand market types and their features

activities_projects.title: Mini Quiz on Markets
steps: Prepare 10 simple questions from the chapter
Ask classmates to answer them
Keep score for correct answers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students reinforce knowledge through peer learning
